original_hypothesis.title: GaugeFactoryCL.setReferralFee can be set above denominator causing GaugeCL.claimFees underflow revert and permanent fee-distribution DoS
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GaugeFactoryCL.setReferralFee has no upper bound. In GaugeCL._claimFees, dibs fee is computed as claimed * dibsPercentage / 1000 and then subtracted from claimed. If dibsPercentage > 1000, _dibsFeeTokenX > claimedX causing underflow revert in Solidity 0.8, blocking claimFees and therefore internal bribe fee distribution for that gauge.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a clear code-path defect leading to bricked fee distribution if misconfigured (or maliciously configured by privileged role). While it requires privileged access (owner/GAUGE_ADMIN), it is a real operational risk with high blast radius for CL fee routing.

proof_of_signal.pattern: dibsPercentage = _dibsFee; no bounds check vs REFERRAL_FEE_DENOMINATOR=1000 used in GaugeCL

preconditions: Caller has onlyAllowed privileges (owner or GAUGE_ADMIN in PermissionsRegistry)
There exist CL gauges with fees to distribute

initial_code_evidence.suspicious_location: GaugeFactoryCL.sol setReferralFee and GaugeCL.sol _claimFees fee subtraction
initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: unbounded BPS value used in subtraction

analysis_questions: Is there any off-chain governance constraint preventing referralFee > 1000?
Do CL gauges have a recovery path if fee distribution is bricked (e.g., change dibsPercentage back)?
validation_requirements: Test setting dibsPercentage to 1001 and calling GaugeCL.claimFees to confirm revert
Verify who controls GAUGE_ADMIN and owner and whether multi-sig/controls exist (out of scope for code, but impacts realism)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A compromised GAUGE_ADMIN sets dibsPercentage to 5000. Subsequent fee claims revert due to underflow, preventing all internal bribe fee notifications for CL pools until governance intervenes.
prerequisites: Privileged role compromise or operator error

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Fee distribution halted; fees may accumulate in gauge until fixed
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: veNFT voters expecting internal bribe fee rewards for CL pools
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Set referral fee back below or equal to 1000

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No bounds tests for referral fee configuration

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Monitor dibsPercentage changes and claimFees reverts

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
